ticket_title: Lock/Unlock IMEI API returns BadRequest for valid state transitions

test_cases.name: TC-1: Lock a currently-unlocked IMEI — happy path [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: IMEI 999464278370433 is in unlocked state in QA env. User has `AccessLevels.AdjustSerializedInventory`. | Test Data: IMEI = 999464278370433 (unlocked), role = Store Manager, storeType = COR, env = platform-mbyt-qa.rt2.com

steps.action: Authenticate as a user with AdjustSerializedInventory access.
POST to `/api/sales/devices/999464278370433/lock`.
Inspect the HTTP response status code and body.
steps.expected: API returns 2xx and the IMEI state transitions to locked.

test_cases.name: TC-2: Unlock a currently-locked IMEI — happy path [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: IMEI 999457851018692 is in locked state in QA env. User has `AccessLevels.AdjustSerializedInventory`. | Test Data: IMEI = 999457851018692 (locked), role = Store Manager, storeType = COR, env = platform-mbyt-qa.rt2.com

steps.action: Authenticate as a user with AdjustSerializedInventory access.
POST to `/api/sales/devices/999457851018692/unlock`.
Inspect the HTTP response status code and body.
steps.expected: API returns 2xx and the IMEI state transitions to unlocked.

test_cases.name: TC-3: Manual lock releases HotDeviceRegistry cache claim [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: IMEI has an active HotDeviceRegistry claim (simulating an in-flight or crashed activation). IMEI is currently unlocked. | Test Data: IMEI = valid 15-digit unlocked IMEI with active cache claim, role = Store Manager, env = QA

steps.action: Confirm via logs or cache inspection that the IMEI has an active HotDeviceRegistry claim.
POST to `/api/sales/devices/{imei}/lock` as authorized user.
Verify the response is successful (2xx).
Inspect HotDeviceRegistry cache to confirm the claim entry is released.
steps.expected: API returns success and the HotDeviceRegistry cache entry for the IMEI is cleared.

test_cases.name: TC-4: Unauthorized role is rejected by lock/unlock endpoints [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Authenticated as a Store Rep without `AccessLevels.AdjustSerializedInventory`. | Test Data: IMEI = valid 15-digit IMEI, role = Store Rep (no AdjustSerializedInventory), storeType = COR

steps.action: Authenticate as a Store Rep without AdjustSerializedInventory access.
POST to `/api/sales/devices/{imei}/lock`.
Inspect the response status and error message.
steps.expected: API returns 403 Forbidden with a clear insufficient-permissions error; no state change occurs.

test_cases.name: TC-5: IMEI format validation — non-15-digit values rejected [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Authenticated as authorized user with AdjustSerializedInventory. | Test Data: IMEI-short = "12345" (5 digits), IMEI-long = "9994642783704330000" (19 digits), role = Store Manager

steps.action: Authenticate as authorized user.
POST to `/api/sales/devices/12345/lock`.
Inspect the response status and error body.
POST to `/api/sales/devices/9994642783704330000/lock`.
Inspect the response status and error body.
steps.expected: Both requests return 400 with a validation error indicating IMEI must be exactly 15 digits.

test_cases.name: TC-6: Audit log entry written on manual lock and unlock [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Authorized user with AdjustSerializedInventory; IMEI in valid unlocked state. | Test Data: IMEI = 999464278370433, role = Store Manager, timestamp = test execution time

steps.action: Authenticate and POST to `/api/sales/devices/999464278370433/lock`.
Confirm response is successful (2xx).
Query the application audit log for the lock event on this IMEI.
steps.expected: An audit log entry exists with user ID, timestamp, IMEI, and outcome (success).
